---
title: "Supplementary Information 1"
subtitle: "Assessing ecosystem effects on the stock productivity of Antarctic Krill (*Euphausia superba*): An Integrated modeling perspective"
author: "Mardones, M; Jarvis Mason, E.T.; Piñones, A.; Krüger, L.,Santa-Cruz, F.; Watters, G., Cárdenas, C.A; Methot, R."
date:  "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
bibliography: SA_krill.bib
csl: apa.csl
link-citations: yes
linkcolor: blue
output:
  bookdown::pdf_document2:
    keep_md: true
    toc: true
    toc_deep: 3
    toc_float:
      collapsed: false
      smooth_scroll: false
    theme: simplex
    fontsize: 0.9em
    linestretch: 1.7
    html-math-method: katex
    self-contained: true
    code-tools: true
    number_sections: false
always_allow_html: true
editor_options:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

## MODELING SETTING

```{r setup1, echo=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      fig.align = 'center',
                      fig.pos = "H",
                      fig.width = 6,
                      fig.height = 4,
                      dev = 'jpeg',
                      fig.path = here::here("Figs", ""),
                      dpi = 300,
                      tidy.opts = list(width.cutoff = 50),
                      tidy = TRUE)
options(bitmapType = "cairo")

# ── Output directories ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dir.create(here::here("Figs"),    showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(here::here("outputs"), showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
```

### Libraries

```{r libs, message=FALSE, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
pkgs <- c("r4ss", "ss3diags", "doParallel",
          "tibble", "tidyr", "tidyverse",
          "readxl", "openxlsx", "broom",
          "forecast", "mixR", "lmtest",
          "car", "ggpubr", "ggthemes",
          "ggridges", "ggrepel",
          "kableExtra", "flextable", "here")

instalar <- pkgs[!pkgs %in% installed.packages()]
if (length(instalar) > 0) install.packages(instalar)
invisible(lapply(pkgs, library, character.only = TRUE))
registerDoParallel(8)
```

### Shared constants

All scenario-level constants are defined once here and reused throughout.

```{r constants, echo=TRUE}
# ── Scenario directories ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dirs <- list(
  s1.1 = here("s1.1"),   # baseline: no predator, no env
  s1.2 = here("s1.2"),   # + predator
  s1.3 = here("s1.3"),   # + env variable
  s1.4 = here("s1.4")    # + predator and env variable
)
Figs <- here("Figs")

# ── Color palette (scenario) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
scen_colors <- c("s1.1" = "#ca0020",
                 "s1.2" = "#f4a582",
                 "s1.3" = "#bababa",
                 "s1.4" = "#404040")

# ── Fleet label mapping ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fleet_labels <- c(`1`  = "FISHERYBS",
                  `2`  = "FISHERYEI",
                  `3`  = "FISHERYGS",
                  `4`  = "FISHERYJOIN",
                  `5`  = "FISHERYSSIW",
                  `6`  = "SURVEYBS",
                  `7`  = "SURVEYEI",
                  `8`  = "SURVEYGS",
                  `9`  = "SURVEYJOIN",
                  `10` = "SURVEYSSIW",
                  `11` = "PREDATOR")

fleet_levels <- unname(fleet_labels)

# ── Helper: recode numeric fleet column to named factor ──────────────────────
recode_fleet <- function(x) {
  factor(dplyr::recode(as.character(x), !!!fleet_labels),
         levels = fleet_levels)
}
```

### Code Repository

The repository with file templates by scenario can be found at this
[GitHub author link](https://github.com/MauroMardones/SA_Krill/tree/main/scenarios).

### Run Models

```{r run_models, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE, include=TRUE}
for (d in dirs) {
  r4ss::run(dir = d, exe = "ss_osx",
            skipfinished = FALSE, show_in_console = TRUE)
}
```

### Load model outputs

```{r load_outputs, message=FALSE}
base.model1.1 <- SS_output(dir = dirs$s1.1, covar = TRUE, forecast = TRUE)
base.model1.2 <- SS_output(dir = dirs$s1.2, covar = TRUE, forecast = TRUE)
base.model1.3 <- SS_output(dir = dirs$s1.3, covar = TRUE, forecast = TRUE)
base.model1.4 <- SS_output(dir = dirs$s1.4, covar = TRUE, forecast = TRUE)

base_models <- list(s1.1 = base.model1.1,
                    s1.2 = base.model1.2,
                    s1.3 = base.model1.3,
                    s1.4 = base.model1.4)
```

### Generate SS plots

```{r ss_plots, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE, include=TRUE}
for (nm in names(base_models)) {
  folder_path <- file.path(nm, "plots")
  if (dir.exists(folder_path)) unlink(folder_path, recursive = TRUE)
  SS_plots(base_models[[nm]],
           uncertainty = TRUE, datplot = TRUE,
           png = TRUE, aalresids = FALSE,
           btarg = 0.75, minbthresh = 0.2, forecast = TRUE)
}
```

# OVERVIEW

This study aims to evaluate the impact of ecosystem components—such as
environmental variables and predator-prey interactions—on the
productivity and key population dynamics of *Euphausia superba* in
Subarea 48.1. This code forms part of the supplementary material for
the scientific article titled **"Assessing environmental and predator
impacts on Antarctic Krill (*Euphausia superba*) population dynamics
from an integrated length-to-age assessment model perspective."**

## Spatial dimension of stock assessment

The study focuses on Subarea 48.1 in the Western Antarctic Peninsula,
where most krill fishing occurs and CCAMLR is advancing spatially
refined management. We use five CCAMLR-defined strata to increase
spatial resolution, allowing detection of regional differences in krill
dynamics (Figure \@ref(fig:mapa)). This structure, integrated into the
stock assessment model, enhances its capacity to capture spatial
variability in both biological and environmental data. In this approach,
spatial structure is incorporated implicitly by treating different areas
as separate fleets [@Nielsen2021; @Waterhouse2014].

```{r mapa, fig.cap="Subarea 48.1 and management strata considered in the spatio-temporal analysis of intrinsic productivity of Krill (BS=Brainsfield Strait, EI= Elephant Island, Gerlache= Gerlache strait, JOIN= Joinville Island, SSWI= South West)"}
knitr::include_graphics('Figs/481.png')
```

Figure \@ref(fig:conceptual) illustrates a conceptual model with the
spatial distribution and movement of Antarctic krill in the Western
Antarctic Peninsula (WAP).

\pagebreak

\begin{landscape}
```{r conceptual, out.width="50%", fig.cap="Conceptual model used to model dynamics population in Antarctic krill in WAP"}
knitr::include_graphics('Figs/conceptual.jpeg')
```
\end{landscape}

\pagebreak

## Statistical Model (`SS3`)

Stock Synthesis (v.3.30.21) is a widely used tool for assessing fish
and invertebrate populations, including Antarctic krill. SS3 is
implemented in `C++` with estimation enabled through automatic
differentiation (ADMB) [@Fournier2012; @Methot2013]. The source code
can be found in the
[Github SS3 repository](https://github.com/nmfs-ost/ss3-source-code).
The analysis of model outputs is conducted using R, utilizing the
*r4ss* and *ss3diags* packages [@Taylor2019; @Winker2023].

## Data

### Parameters

```{r parainit1, eval=TRUE}
start1 <- SS_readstarter(file = file.path(dirs$s1.1, "starter.ss"),
                         verbose = FALSE)
dat1   <- SS_readdat(file = file.path(dirs$s1.1, start1$datfile),
                     verbose = FALSE)
ctl1   <- SS_readctl(file = file.path(dirs$s1.1, start1$ctlfil),
                     verbose = FALSE, use_datlist = TRUE, datlist = dat1)

parbio <- ctl1$MG_parms[1:10, c(1:4, 7)]
rownames(parbio) <- c("Nat M", "Lmin", "Lmax", "VonBert K",
                      "CV young", "CV old", "Wt a", "Wt b",
                      "L50%", "Mat slope")
SRpar  <- ctl1$SR_parms[1:5, c(1:4, 7)]
Qpar   <- ctl1$Q_parms[1:2, c(1:4, 7)]
Selpar <- ctl1$size_selex_parms[1:20, c(1:4, 7)]

parInit <- as.data.frame(rbind(parbio, SRpar, Qpar, Selpar)) %>%
  mutate(INIT = round(INIT, 2), PRIOR = round(PRIOR, 2),
         Parameter = rownames(.),
         Category = c(rep("Natural Mortality", 1),
                      rep("Growth", 4),
                      rep("Length-Weight Relation", 2),
                      rep("Maturity", 2),
                      rep("Stock-Recruit Relation", 5),
                      rep("Catchability", 2),
                      rep("Selectivity", 21))) %>%
  dplyr::relocate(Category, Parameter)

ft <- flextable(parInit)
ft <- set_caption(ft,
  caption = as_paragraph(
    "Input parameters for the initial SS3 model of krill. Each parameter line
     contains a minimum value (LO), maximum value (HI), and initial value (INIT).
     If the phase (PHASE) is negative, the parameter is fixed as input"),
  autonum = NULL)
ft <- autofit(ft)
ft <- theme_booktabs(ft)
ft <- set_table_properties(ft, width = .5, layout = "autofit")
ft <- fontsize(ft, size = 9, part = "all")
ft
```

### Index

Abundance index in Figure \@ref(fig:index).

```{r index_data}
dat1_std <- dat1$CPUE %>%
  mutate(
    index_std = (obs - min(obs)) / (max(obs) - min(obs)),
    index     = recode_fleet(index),
    category  = case_when(
      index %in% c("FISHERYBS", "FISHERYEI", "FISHERYGS",
                   "FISHERYJOIN", "FISHERYSSIW") ~ "Fishery",
      index %in% c("SURVEYBS", "SURVEYEI", "SURVEYGS",
                   "SURVEYJOIN", "SURVEYSSIW")   ~ "Survey",
      index == "PREDATOR"                        ~ "Predator",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )
```

```{r index, fig.cap="Standardized indices of krill abundance and consumption from fishery-dependent, fishery-independent, and predator-based data sources across different strata within Subarea 48.1."}
ggplot(dat1_std, aes(x = year, y = index_std, group = index, color = category)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~index, scales = "free_y", ncol = 5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Fishery" = '#1b9e77',
                                "Survey"  = '#d95f02',
                                "Predator"= '#7570b3')) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Standard index", color = "Category")
ggsave(here("Figs", "index_std.png"), width = 10, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

### Length compositions

```{r lencomp_data, warning=FALSE}
dat_lencomp <- dat1$lencomp

# Normalize fleet column name across r4ss versions
posibles_fleet <- c("FltSvy", "Fleet", "fleet", "Flt", "flt",
                    "FLT", "FLT_SVY", "fltsvy")
col_flota <- intersect(posibles_fleet, names(dat_lencomp))
if (length(col_flota) == 0) {
  col_flota <- names(dat_lencomp)[3]
  message("AVISO: columna de flota no detectada por nombre. Usando columna 3: '",
          col_flota, "'")
}
if (col_flota[1] != "FltSvy")
  dat_lencomp <- dplyr::rename(dat_lencomp, FltSvy = !!rlang::sym(col_flota[1]))

# Normalize year column (r4ss >= 1.46 uses "year")
if (!"Yr" %in% names(dat_lencomp) && "year" %in% names(dat_lencomp))
  dat_lencomp <- dplyr::rename(dat_lencomp, Yr = year)

dat_long <- dat_lencomp %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("l"),
               names_to = "Talla", values_to = "Frecuencia") %>%
  mutate(Talla  = as.numeric(sub("l", "", Talla)),
         FltSvy = recode_fleet(FltSvy))

mean_talla   <- dat_long %>%
  group_by(FltSvy) %>%
  summarise(mean_Talla = mean(Talla, na.rm = TRUE))

dat_expanded <- dat_long %>% uncount(weights = Frecuencia)
```

Length compositions in Figure \@ref(fig:length).

```{r length, warning=FALSE, fig.cap="Annual length-frequency distributions of Antarctic krill across strata and data sources within Subarea 48.1. The vertical line marks the recruit reference length (3.6 cm)."}
fleet_fill <- c(
  FISHERYBS = '#1b9e77', FISHERYEI = '#1b9e77', FISHERYGS = '#1b9e77',
  FISHERYJOIN = '#1b9e77', FISHERYSSIW = '#1b9e77',
  SURVEYBS = '#d95f02', SURVEYEI = '#d95f02', SURVEYGS = '#d95f02',
  SURVEYJOIN = '#d95f02', SURVEYSSIW = '#d95f02',
  PREDATOR = '#7570b3')

ggplot(dat_expanded, aes(x = Talla, y = as.factor(Yr), fill = FltSvy)) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(scale = 1.8, rel_min_height = 0.001,
                               col = "black", alpha = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(~FltSvy, ncol = 10) +
  geom_vline(data = mean_talla, aes(xintercept = mean_Talla),
             color = "red", size = 0.7, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fleet_fill) +
  labs(x = "cm", y = "") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
ggsave(here("Figs", "length_comps.png"), width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

## Summary data sources


The modeling framework pathway is shown in Figure \@ref(fig:path).
```{r path, fig.cap="Framework path to stock assessment model in krill in WAP (Yellow boxes not implemented)."}
knitr::include_graphics('Figs/pathmod.png')
```


Figure \@ref(fig:dataserie) summarizes the data series used as model input.

```{r dataserie, fig.cap="Data series used in krill modelling in 48.1 Subarea"}
SSplotData(base.model1.4, subplots = 1, ptsize = -2)
```

## Scenarios

The **reference model** is `s1.1`, representing a baseline assessment
excluding environmental and ecological variables.

```{r scenarios, eval=TRUE}
data.frame(
  Scenario = c("s1.1", "s1.2", "s1.3", "s1.4"),
  Description = c(
    "Spatial data without environmental and predator components",
    "\"s1.1\" with predator components",
    "\"s1.1\" with environmental variable",
    "\"s1.1\" with both predator fleet and environmental variable")) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Scenarios used for modelling dynamics in krill",
      align = "l", booktabs = TRUE,
      col.names = c("Scenario", "Description")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE, position = "center", font_size = 10,
                latex_options = "HOLD_position") %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, color = "black") %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "2cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "10cm")
```

# RESULTS

## Population Variables by scenario

### ALK matrix


Figure \@ref(fig:AKL) shows the age–length key (ALK) matrix for the baseline scenario.
```{r AKL, fig.cap="Representation of ALK Matrix for krill in 48.1"}
alk_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(base.model1.1$ALK[,,1]))
colnames(alk_df) <- c("Length", "TrueAge", "Value")
alk_df$Length   <- as.numeric(as.character(alk_df$Length))
alk_df$TrueAge  <- as.numeric(as.character(alk_df$TrueAge))
alk_df$Value    <- as.numeric(alk_df$Value)

ggplot(alk_df, aes(x = TrueAge, y = Length, fill = Value)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "blue") +
  labs(x = "True Age", y = "Length", fill = "Value") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
ggsave(here("Figs", "ALK_matrix.png"), width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

### Time series per scenario

Main population variables by scenario (Figures \@ref(fig:scen1)–\@ref(fig:scen4)).

```{r scen1, fig.cap="Main variables scenario s1.1"}
par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(5, 4, 1, 1))
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.1, subplot = 1, xlab = "")
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.1, subplot = 9, xlab = "")
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.1, subplot = 7, xlab = "")
```

```{r scen2, fig.cap="Main variables scenario s1.2"}
par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(5, 4, 1, 1))
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.2, subplot = 1, xlab = "")
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.2, subplot = 9, xlab = "")
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.2, subplot = 7, xlab = "")
```

```{r scen3, fig.cap="Main variables scenario s1.3"}
par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(5, 4, 1, 1))
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.3, subplot = 1, xlab = "")
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.3, subplot = 9, xlab = "")
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.3, subplot = 7, xlab = "")
```

```{r scen4, fig.cap="Main variables scenario s1.4"}
par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(5, 4, 1, 1))
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.4, subplot = 1, xlab = "")
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.4, subplot = 9, xlab = "")
SSplotTimeseries(base.model1.4, subplot = 7, xlab = "")
```


Selectivity curves by fleet and scenario are shown in Figure \@ref(fig:selectivity).
```{r selectivity, fig.cap="Selectivity by fleet in each scenario"}
par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(5, 4, 1, 1))
for (nm in names(base_models)) {
  SSplotSelex(base_models[[nm]], subplots = 1, mainTitle = FALSE)
  mtext(nm, side = 3, line = 0.5, cex = 1.2)
}
```


Fitted indices by scenario are presented in Figure \@ref(fig:index2).
```{r index2, fig.cap="Standardized indices used as input in four model scenarios (s1.1 to s1.4)."}
par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(5, 4, 2, 1))
for (nm in names(base_models)) {
  SSplotIndices(base_models[[nm]], subplots = 9)
  mtext(nm, side = 3, line = 0.5, cex = 1.2)
}
```

### Output time-series tables

```{r output_tables, results='asis'}
for (nm in names(base_models)) {
  cat(sprintf("\n\n#### Outputs Variables `%s`\n\n", nm))
  tbl <- base_models[[nm]]$timeseries[1:37, 2:8]
  print(
    kbl(tbl, booktabs = TRUE, format = "latex",
        caption = sprintf("Main variables outputs from stock assessment krill in WAP `%s`", nm)) %>%
      kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down", "striped"), font_size = 8)
  )
}
```

### Comparison between variables


Figure \@ref(fig:popvar2) presents the time series of population variables by scenario.
```{r popvar2, fig.cap="Time series of population variables by scenario"}
# Add Model column before binding (avoids the previous double-rbind bug)
outpsall <- map_dfr(names(base_models), function(nm) {
  base_models[[nm]]$timeseries[1:37, 2:8] %>%
    mutate(Model = nm)
})

outpsall_long <- outpsall %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Bio_all, Bio_smry, SpawnBio, Recruit_0),
               names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Value") %>%
  filter(Yr >= 1989, Yr <= 2020)

ggplot(outpsall_long, aes(x = Yr, y = Value, color = Model, group = Model)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~Variable, ncol = 4, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Scenario", values = scen_colors) +
  labs(x = "", y = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom")
ggsave(here("Figs", "popvar_scenarios.png"), width = 8, height = 3, dpi = 300)
```


Total biomass trajectories by scenario are shown in Figure \@ref(fig:totbio_plot).
```{r totbio_plot, fig.cap="Total biomass estimated by scenario — Subarea 48.1"}
totbio <- map_dfr(
  base_models,
  ~ .x$timeseries[, c("Yr", "Bio_all", "Bio_smry", "SpawnBio", "Recruit_0")],
  .id = "Model")

totbio %>%
  dplyr::filter(Yr >= 1991) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Yr, y = Bio_all / 1e6, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Total biomass (Mt)", color = "Scenario") +
  scale_color_manual(values = scen_colors) +
  theme_bw()
```

Difference between scenarios regarding summary population variables in
Table \@ref(tab:varcompar).

```{r varcompar}
prom_hist <- outpsall %>%
  group_by(Model) %>%
  summarise(Bio_all_hist    = mean(Bio_all / 1e6, na.rm = TRUE),
            Recruit_0_hist  = mean(Recruit_0 / 1e6, na.rm = TRUE))

ult_5 <- outpsall %>%
  group_by(Model) %>%
  filter(Yr %in% sort(unique(Yr), decreasing = TRUE)[1:5]) %>%
  summarise(Bio_all_5yr   = mean(Bio_all / 1e6, na.rm = TRUE),
            Recruit_0_5yr = mean(Recruit_0 / 1e6, na.rm = TRUE))

base_vals <- filter(prom_hist, Model == "s1.1")

resumen <- left_join(prom_hist, ult_5, by = "Model") %>%
  mutate(
    Bio_all_hist_pct   = 100 * (Bio_all_hist   - base_vals$Bio_all_hist)   / base_vals$Bio_all_hist,
    Recruit_0_hist_pct = 100 * (Recruit_0_hist - base_vals$Recruit_0_hist) / base_vals$Recruit_0_hist,
    Bio_all_5yr_pct    = 100 * (Bio_all_5yr    - base_vals$Bio_all_5yr)    / base_vals$Bio_all_5yr,
    Recruit_0_5yr_pct  = 100 * (Recruit_0_5yr  - base_vals$Recruit_0_5yr)  / base_vals$Recruit_0_5yr
  )

resumen %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2))) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Summary comparison across scenarios") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE,
                bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
```

```{r save_varcompar, echo=FALSE}
write.csv(resumen, here("outputs", "summary_popvars.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```

### Scenario comparisons (SSplotComparisons)

```{r ssplot_compare_all, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
biglist <- SSgetoutput(keyvec = NULL,
                       dirvec = unname(dirs),
                       getcovar = FALSE)
summaryoutput <- SSsummarize(biglist)

SSplotComparisons(summaryoutput,
                  legendlabels = c("s1.1 (Ref Model)", "s1.2", "s1.3", "s1.4"),
                  filenameprefix = "COM1",
                  labels = c("Year", "Spawning biomass (t)",
                             "Relative spawning biomass",
                             "Age-0 recruits (1,000s)",
                             "Recruitment deviations", "Index", "Log index",
                             "1 - SPR", "Density", "Management target",
                             "Minimum stock size threshold",
                             "Spawning output", "Harvest rate"),
                  png = TRUE, plotdir = Figs)
```

### R0 comparison


The estimated R0 distributions by scenario are shown in Figure \@ref(fig:r0).
```{r r0, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="R0 probability predicted by scenario"}
r0_vals <- map_dfr(names(base_models), function(nm) {
  idx <- if (nm %in% c("s1.2", "s1.4")) 2L else 1L
  data.frame(model = nm,
             value = base_models[[nm]]$jitter_info[idx, 1],
             cv    = base_models[[nm]]$jitter_info[idx, 6])
})

x_vals <- seq(10, 30, length.out = 1000)

dens_data <- r0_vals %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(density = list(data.frame(
    x = x_vals, y = dnorm(x_vals, mean = value, sd = cv), model = model))) %>%
  dplyr::select(density) %>%
  unnest(cols = c(density))

ggplot(dens_data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = model, fill = model)) +
  geom_area(alpha = 0.3, position = "identity", size = 0) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = scen_colors, name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = scen_colors, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = "ln(R0)", y = "Density") +
  xlim(20, 30) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
ggsave(here("Figs", "R0_distributions.png"), width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

### Cumulative comparison


Figure \@ref(fig:cumsum) shows the distribution of population variables across scenarios.
```{r cumsum, fig.cap="Comparison of population variable distributions across scenarios"}
err_long <- map_dfr(names(base_models), function(nm) {
  ts <- base_models[[nm]]$timeseries
  data.frame(
    Bio_all  = ts$Bio_all,
    Bio_smry = ts$Bio_smry,
    SpawnBio = ts$SpawnBio,
    Recruit_0 = ts$Recruit_0,
    Model    = nm
  )
}) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Bio_all, Bio_smry, SpawnBio, Recruit_0),
               names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Value")

comsum <- ggplot(err_long, aes(x = Model, y = Value, fill = Model)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~Variable, ncol = 4, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Scenario", values = scen_colors) +
  labs(x = "", y = "") +
  theme_few() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")

comsum
ggsave(filename = "Figs/COMSUM.png", plot = comsum,
       width = 10, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

## Autocorrelation in Recruitment


Autocorrelation in recruitment by scenario is presented in Figure \@ref(fig:acf).
```{r acf, fig.cap="Autocorrelation in recruitment by scenario"}
acf_plots <- imap(base_models, ~ {
  ggAcf(.x$recruit$pred_recr) + ggtitle(.y) + theme_few()
})
p_acf <- ggarrange(plotlist = acf_plots, ncol = 4, nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE)
p_acf
ggsave(here("Figs", "ACF_recruitment.png"), p_acf, width = 12, height = 3, dpi = 300)
```

## Relationship Stock-Recruit

\[
R = \frac{R_0 \cdot S}{S_0 (1 - h) + S (5h - 1)}
\]

```{r srr_calc}
shapes <- c("s1.1" = 5, "s1.2" = 6, "s1.3" = 15, "s1.4" = 4)

plot_data  <- list()
curve_data <- list()
label_data <- list()

for (nm in names(base_models)) {
  model <- base_models[[nm]]
  l  <- model$recruit$SpawnBio
  m  <- model$recruit$pred_recr
  yr <- model$timeseries$Yr[1:35]

  valid <- which(is.finite(l) & is.finite(m) & l > 0 & m > 0)
  l  <- l[valid]; m <- m[valid]; yr <- yr[valid]

  plot_data[[nm]]  <- data.frame(l = l, m = m, yr = yr, Group = nm)
  label_data[[nm]] <- filter(plot_data[[nm]], yr %% 3 == 0)

  tryCatch({
    fit    <- nls(m ~ (a * l) / (1 + b * l),
                  start = list(a = max(m), b = 1 / max(l)),
                  data  = data.frame(l, m))
    l_pred <- seq(min(l), max(l), length.out = 100)
    curve_data[[nm]] <- data.frame(
      l = l_pred, m = predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(l = l_pred)), Group = nm)
  }, error = function(e)
    message("Beverton-Holt fit failed for ", nm, ": ", e$message))
}

plot_data_all  <- bind_rows(plot_data)
curve_data_all <- bind_rows(curve_data)
label_data_all <- bind_rows(label_data)

srr_sce <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = plot_data_all,
             aes(x = l, y = m, shape = Group), size = 2) +
  geom_line(data = curve_data_all,
            aes(x = l, y = m, color = Group), linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text_repel(data = label_data_all,
                  aes(x = l, y = m, label = yr, color = Group),
                  size = 4, max.overlaps = 50) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = shapes) +
  scale_color_manual(values = scen_colors) +
  labs(x = "SSB", y = "Predicted Recruitment",
       shape = "Model", color = "Model") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

## Productivity and Interannual Variability

```{r productivity_calc}
resultados <- imap_dfr(base_models, function(mod, nm) {
  mod$SPAWN_RECR_CURVE %>%
    mutate(Scenario     = nm,
           Productividad = Recruitment / SSB,
           Cambio_Recruit = c(NA, diff(Recruitment)) / lag(Recruitment) * 100,
           Cambio_SSB     = c(NA, diff(SSB)) / lag(SSB) * 100)
})

prod <- ggplot(resultados, aes(x = `SSB/SSB_virgin`, y = Productividad,
                               color = Scenario)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.1) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "", values = scen_colors) +
  labs(y = "Recruitment / SSB", x = "SSB / SSB_virgin") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```


Stock–recruitment relationships and productivity are shown in Figure \@ref(fig:recpro).
```{r recpro, fig.cap="Left: Stock–recruitment relationships. Right: Recruitment per unit SSB vs. relative SSB."}
p_recpro <- ggarrange(srr_sce, prod, ncol = 2)
p_recpro
ggsave(here("Figs", "SRR_productivity.png"), p_recpro, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

## Exploitation Rate


Exploitation rates over time by scenario are presented in Figure \@ref(fig:hrate).
```{r hrate, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="Harvest rate by scenario over time"}
df_exploit <- map_dfr(names(base_models), function(nm) {
  base_models[[nm]]$exploitation[, c(1, 4)] %>%
    mutate(model = nm)
})
colnames(df_exploit) <- c("year", "exploitation_rate", "model")

ggplot(df_exploit, aes(x = year, y = exploitation_rate, color = model)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 3, shape = 16) +
  scale_color_manual(values = scen_colors) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1998, 2020, by = 1)) +
  xlim(1997, 2020) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Exploitation Rate", color = "Model") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1))
ggsave(here("Figs", "harvest_rate.png"), width = 9, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

## Diagnosis and Robustness

A rigorous model diagnosis is essential to ensure the reliability and
robustness of stock assessment models. Key steps include:

1. **Convergence check** — final criterion of 1.0e-04.
2. **Residual analysis** — visual and statistical evaluation.
3. **Retrospective analysis** — Mohn's rho for consistency.
4. **Likelihood profile analysis** — parameter uncertainty and sensitivity.

This framework follows the recommendations of @Carvalho2021b.

### Residuals


Pearson residuals by scenario and fleet are shown in Figure \@ref(fig:pearson).
```{r pearson, fig.cap="Pearson residuals by scenario and fleet"}
# Helper: normalize column names across r4ss versions
normalize_lendbase <- function(df, model_name) {
  if (!"Yr" %in% names(df) && "year" %in% names(df))
    df <- dplyr::rename(df, Yr = year)
  bin_col  <- intersect(c("Bin", "bin", "Lbin_lo", "lbin_lo"), names(df))[1]
  if (!is.na(bin_col) && bin_col != "Bin")
    df <- dplyr::rename(df, Bin = !!rlang::sym(bin_col))
  pear_col <- intersect(c("Pearson", "pearson"), names(df))[1]
  if (!is.na(pear_col) && pear_col != "Pearson")
    df <- dplyr::rename(df, Pearson = !!rlang::sym(pear_col))
  fleet_col <- intersect(c("Fleet", "FltSvy", "fleet", "Flt"), names(df))[1]
  if (!is.na(fleet_col) && fleet_col != "Fleet")
    df <- dplyr::rename(df, Fleet = !!rlang::sym(fleet_col))

  df %>%
    dplyr::filter(Pearson < 5) %>%
    dplyr::select(Yr, Bin, Pearson, Fleet) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Fleet = dplyr::recode(as.character(Fleet), !!!fleet_labels),
                  Model = model_name)
}

df_pearson <- map2_dfr(
  base_models,
  paste("Model", c("1.1", "1.2", "1.3", "1.4")),
  ~ normalize_lendbase(.x$lendbase, .y))

ggplot(df_pearson, aes(x = Yr, y = Bin)) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = Pearson, size = abs(Pearson)),
             shape = 21, color = "grey30", stroke = 0.2, alpha = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", mid = "white", high = "blue", midpoint = 0) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(0.5, 2.5)) +
  facet_grid(Model ~ Fleet) +
  theme_few() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 5),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        strip.text  = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  labs(x = "", y = "Length (cm)", fill = "Pearson") +
  guides(size = "none", alpha = "none")
ggsave(here("Figs", "Pearson_residuals.png"), width = 14, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```


Temporal trends in Pearson residuals by fleet and scenario are shown in Figure \@ref(fig:pearson_trend).
```{r pearson_trend, fig.cap="Pearson residual trend by scenario and fleet"}
lm_stats <- df_pearson %>%
  group_by(Model, Fleet) %>%
  do({
    mod <- lm(Pearson ~ Yr, data = .)
    out <- tidy(mod)
    data.frame(
      slope = round(out$estimate[out$term == "Yr"], 3),
      pval  = round(out$p.value[out$term  == "Yr"], 3))
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(df_pearson, aes(x = Yr, y = Pearson)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.7, color = "grey30") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue", linewidth = 0.6) +
  facet_grid(Model ~ Fleet) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Pearson residuals") +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 7)) +
  geom_text(data = lm_stats,
            aes(x = min(df_pearson$Yr) + 2,
                y = max(df_pearson$Pearson, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.3,
                label = paste0("slope=", slope, "\np=", pval)),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 2.5, hjust = 0)
ggsave(here("Figs", "Pearson_trend.png"), width = 14, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```

\end{landscape}

\pagebreak

```{r build_resid_all}
# Normalize helper reused for both cpue and lendbase
norm_yr   <- function(df) {
  if (!"Yr" %in% names(df) && "year" %in% names(df))
    dplyr::rename(df, Yr = year)
  else df
}
norm_fleet <- function(df) {
  if (!"Fleet" %in% names(df)) {
    fc <- intersect(c("FltSvy", "fleet", "Flt"), names(df))[1]
    if (!is.na(fc)) dplyr::rename(df, Fleet = !!rlang::sym(fc))
    else df
  } else df
}

cpue_resid <- map_dfr(names(base_models),
  ~ norm_yr(base_models[[.x]]$cpue) %>% mutate(model = .x))

len_resid <- map_dfr(names(base_models),
  ~ norm_yr(norm_fleet(base_models[[.x]]$lendbase)) %>% mutate(model = .x))

cpue_df <- cpue_resid %>%
  transmute(Yr, Obs, Exp, model,
            Fleet_name = as.character(Fleet_name), type = "Index")

len_df <- len_resid %>%
  transmute(Yr, Obs, Exp, model,
            Fleet_name = as.character(Fleet), type = "Length")

resid_all <- bind_rows(cpue_df, len_df) %>%
  mutate(Fleet_name = dplyr::recode(Fleet_name, !!!fleet_labels))
```

### Residual consistency


Runs tests for length composition residuals are presented below.
```{r runs_len}
par(mfrow = c(2, 4), mar = c(5, 4, 2, 1))
for (mod in base_models)
  SSplotRunstest(mod, subplots = "len", add = TRUE, plot = TRUE, plotdir = Figs)
```


Runs tests for CPUE residuals are presented below.
```{r runs_cpue}
par(mfrow = c(2, 5), mar = c(5, 4, 2, 1))
for (mod in base_models)
  SSplotRunstest(mod, subplots = "cpue", add = TRUE, plot = TRUE, plotdir = Figs)
```

### Residual Analysis and RMSE


Figure \@ref(fig:rmse1) shows RMSE time series for length compositions by scenario.
```{r rmse1, fig.height=8, fig.width=8, fig.cap="Time series of RMSE of length compositions by scenario"}
par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(5, 4, 2, 1))
rmse_len  <- lapply(base_models, SSplotJABBAres, subplots = "len",  add = TRUE)
```


Figure \@ref(fig:rmse2) shows RMSE time series for CPUE by scenario.
```{r rmse2, fig.height=8, fig.width=8, fig.cap="Time series of RMSE of CPUE by scenario"}
par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(5, 4, 2, 1))
rmse_cpue <- lapply(base_models, SSplotJABBAres, subplots = "cpue", add = TRUE)
```

### Comparison RMSE

```{r rmse_data}
dfrmse <- data.frame(
  s1.1 = as.numeric(base.model1.1$index_variance_tuning_check$RMSE),
  s1.2 = as.numeric(base.model1.2$index_variance_tuning_check$RMSE[1:10]),
  s1.3 = as.numeric(base.model1.3$index_variance_tuning_check$RMSE[1:10]),
  s1.4 = as.numeric(base.model1.4$index_variance_tuning_check$RMSE[1:10]))

dfrmse_long <- dfrmse %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
               names_to = "variable", values_to = "value")

rmse_plot <- ggplot(dfrmse_long, aes(x = variable, y = value, fill = variable)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8, outliers = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.01, alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Scenario", values = scen_colors) +
  labs(x = "", y = "RMSE") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

dfpearson_long <- map_dfr(names(base_models), function(nm) {
  data.frame(Pearson  = base_models[[nm]]$lendbase$Pearson,
             Scenario = nm)
})

pearson_plot <- ggplot(dfpearson_long, aes(x = Scenario, y = Pearson, fill = Scenario)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8, outliers = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Scenario", values = scen_colors) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Residual") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```


Figure \@ref(fig:rmse3) compares RMSE and Pearson residual distributions across scenarios.
```{r rmse3, fig.cap="RMSE and Pearson residuals by scenario"}
ggarrange(rmse_plot, pearson_plot, ncol = 2)
```

### Retrospective Analysis

```{r retro_run, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
for (d in dirs) {
  retro(dir = d, oldsubdir = "", newsubdir = "Retrospective",
        years = 0:-5, exe = "ss_osx",
        extras = "-nox", skipfinished = FALSE)
}
```

```{r retro_load, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
retro_summaries <- lapply(dirs, function(d) {
  models <- SSgetoutput(
    dirvec = file.path(d, "Retrospective", paste0("retro", 0:-4)))
  SSsummarize(models)
})
# Convenience aliases
retroSummary1.1 <- retro_summaries$s1.1
retroSummary1.2 <- retro_summaries$s1.2
retroSummary1.3 <- retro_summaries$s1.3
retroSummary1.4 <- retro_summaries$s1.4
```


Retrospective patterns for spawning biomass are shown in Figure \@ref(fig:retrossb).
```{r retrossb, fig.cap="Retrospective analysis for spawning biomass by scenario"}
par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(5, 4, 1, 1))
for (rs in retro_summaries)
  SSplotRetro(rs, add = TRUE, forecast = FALSE,
              legend = TRUE, verbose = FALSE, subplots = "SSB")
```


Retrospective patterns for fishing mortality are shown in Figure \@ref(fig:retrof).
```{r retrof, fig.cap="Retrospective analysis for fishing mortality by scenario"}
par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(5, 4, 1, 1))
for (rs in retro_summaries)
  SSplotRetro(rs, add = TRUE, forecast = FALSE,
              legend = TRUE, verbose = FALSE, subplots = "F")
```

```{r rho_tables}
# Build combined Rho table across all scenarios and quantities
tablebias_list <- lapply(names(retro_summaries), function(nm) {
  rs <- retro_summaries[[nm]]
  bind_rows(
    data.frame(Model = nm, Quant = "SSB",
               SShcbias(rs, quant = "SSB", verbose = FALSE)),
    data.frame(Model = nm, Quant = "F",
               SShcbias(rs, quant = "F",   verbose = FALSE))
  )
})

tablebias_combined <- bind_rows(tablebias_list) %>%
  mutate(Rho = factor(Rho, levels = unique(Rho)))

# Per-scenario individual tables (SSB and F)
for (nm in names(retro_summaries)) {
  rs <- retro_summaries[[nm]]
  cat(sprintf("\n\n#### Rho — %s\n\n", nm))
  print(kbl(SShcbias(rs, quant = "SSB", verbose = FALSE),
            booktabs = TRUE, format = "latex",
            caption = sprintf("Rho SSB — %s", nm)) %>%
          kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position"))
  print(kbl(SShcbias(rs, quant = "F", verbose = FALSE),
            booktabs = TRUE, format = "latex",
            caption = sprintf("Rho F — %s", nm)) %>%
          kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position"))
}
```


Retrospective bias (Rho) by scenario and quantity is shown in Figure \@ref(fig:bias).
```{r bias, fig.cap="Retrospective bias (Rho) in SSB and F by scenario and peel year"}
ggplot(tablebias_combined,
       aes(y = Rho, group = Model, fill = Model)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey") +
  facet_wrap(~Quant, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Scenario", values = scen_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Scenario", values = scen_colors) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Rho Value") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom")
ggsave(here("Figs", "retrospective_rho.png"), width = 8, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

```{r rhoparameters}
kbl(tablebias_combined,
    booktabs = TRUE, format = "latex",
    caption = "Rho parameter by model and quantity (SSB and F)") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position")
```

### Hindcast Cross-Validation and Prediction Skill


Hindcast cross-validation results by scenario are shown in Figure \@ref(fig:hcval_all).
```{r hcval_all, fig.cap="Hindcast validation by scenario", results='asis'}
hcval_list <- list()
for (nm in names(retro_summaries)) {
  cat(sprintf("\n\n#### Hindcast — `%s`\n\n", nm))
  par(mfrow = c(3, 3), mar = c(5, 4, 2, 1))
  hcval_list[[nm]] <- SSplotHCxval(retro_summaries[[nm]],
                                   add = TRUE, verbose = FALSE,
                                   legendcex = 0.7)
}
```

### Model Diagnostics Summary

```{r diag_build}
# Helper: extract all diagnostics for one scenario
make_diag <- function(nm, mod, rmse_c, rmse_l, hci, bias_ssb, bias_f) {
  data.frame(
    Scenario          = nm,
    Convergency       = mod$maximum_gradient_component,
    AIC               = as.numeric(
      2 * dim(mod$estimated_non_dev_parameters)[1] +
      2 * mod$likelihoods_used[1, 1]),
    Total_like        = mod$likelihoods_used$values[
      rownames(mod$likelihoods_used) == "TOTAL"],
    N_Params          = as.numeric(
      2 * dim(mod$estimated_non_dev_parameters)[1]),
    Survey_like       = mod$likelihoods_used$values[
      rownames(mod$likelihoods_used) == "Survey"],
    Length_comp_like  = mod$likelihoods_used$values[
      rownames(mod$likelihoods_used) == "Length_comp"],
    RMSE_index        = rmse_c$RMSE.perc[rmse_c$indices == "Combined"],
    RMSE_length       = rmse_l$RMSE.perc[rmse_l$indices == "Combined"],
    MASE              = mean(hci$MAE.base, na.rm = TRUE),
    Retro_Rho_ssb     = bias_ssb[5, 3],
    Forecast_Rho_ssb  = bias_ssb[5, 4],
    Forecast_Rho_f    = bias_f[5, 3],
    Rho_f             = bias_f[5, 4])
}

# Unpack per-scenario objects from previously computed lists
diag_all <- bind_rows(
  make_diag("s1.1", base.model1.1,
            rmse_cpue[[1]], rmse_len[[1]], hcval_list[["s1.1"]],
            SShcbias(retroSummary1.1, quant = "SSB", verbose = FALSE),
            SShcbias(retroSummary1.1, quant = "F",   verbose = FALSE)),
  make_diag("s1.2", base.model1.2,
            rmse_cpue[[2]], rmse_len[[2]], hcval_list[["s1.2"]],
            SShcbias(retroSummary1.2, quant = "SSB", verbose = FALSE),
            SShcbias(retroSummary1.2, quant = "F",   verbose = FALSE)),
  make_diag("s1.3", base.model1.3,
            rmse_cpue[[3]], rmse_len[[3]], hcval_list[["s1.3"]],
            SShcbias(retroSummary1.3, quant = "SSB", verbose = FALSE),
            SShcbias(retroSummary1.3, quant = "F",   verbose = FALSE)),
  make_diag("s1.4", base.model1.4,
            rmse_cpue[[4]], rmse_len[[4]], hcval_list[["s1.4"]],
            SShcbias(retroSummary1.4, quant = "SSB", verbose = FALSE),
            SShcbias(retroSummary1.4, quant = "F",   verbose = FALSE))
)

diag_tidy <- diag_all %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Scenario, names_to = "Description", values_to = "Value") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Scenario, values_from = Value)

diag_tidy[, 2:5] <- lapply(diag_tidy[, 2:5],
  function(x) as.numeric(format(round(x, 3), nsmall = 3)))
write.csv(diag_tidy, here("outputs", "model_diagnostics.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r likecom}
diag_tidy %>%
  kbl(digits = 3, caption = "Model Diagnosis Results",
      align = "c", booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE, position = "center") %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)
```

### Parameter and Likelihood Comparison

```{r parametercomparison, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
biglist       <- SSgetoutput(keyvec = NULL, dirvec = unname(dirs), getcovar = FALSE)
summaryoutput <- SSsummarize(biglist)

tablelike <- SStableComparisons(
  summaryoutput,
  likenames  = c("TOTAL", "Survey", "Length_comp", "Age_comp",
                 "priors", "Size_at_age"),
  names      = c("Recr_Virgin", "R0", "SSB_Virg", "Bratio_2020", "SPRratio_2020"),
  digits     = NULL,
  modelnames = names(dirs),
  csv        = TRUE,
  csvdir     = here(),
  csvfile    = "parameter_comparison_table.csv",
  verbose    = TRUE, mcmc = FALSE)

tablelike %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric),
                ~ format(.x, scientific = FALSE, digits = 4))) %>%
  dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.)))) %>%
  kbl(booktabs = TRUE, format = "latex",
      caption = "Model parameter and likelihood comparison") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = "scale_down")
```

### Total Likelihood Composition


Likelihood composition by scenario is shown in Figure \@ref(fig:likecompo2).
```{r likecompo2, fig.cap="Total likelihood composition by scenario"}
like_df <- map_dfr(names(base_models), function(nm) {
  base_models[[nm]]$likelihoods_used %>%
    dplyr::select(values) %>%
    mutate(Componente = rownames(base_models[[nm]]$likelihoods_used),
           Modelo = nm)
})

ggplot(like_df %>% filter(values > 1),
       aes(x = Componente, y = values, fill = Modelo)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(), width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Scenario", values = scen_colors) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Log-Normal Likelihood") +
  theme_few()
ggsave(here("Figs", "likelihood_components.png"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

### Statistical Differences Between Models

```{r residual_stats, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
resid_all <- resid_all %>% mutate(residual = Obs - Exp)

resid_stats <- resid_all %>%
  group_by(type, model) %>%
  summarise(N    = n(),
            Mean = mean(residual, na.rm = TRUE),
            SD   = sd(residual,   na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

shapiro_p <- resid_all %>%
  group_by(type, model) %>%
  summarise(
    shapiro_p = ifelse(n() > 3, shapiro.test(residual)$p.value, NA),
    .groups = "drop")

ljung_p <- resid_all %>%
  group_by(type, model) %>%
  summarise(
    ljung_p = ifelse(n() > 10,
                     Box.test(residual, lag = 10, type = "Ljung-Box")$p.value, NA),
    .groups = "drop")

bp_p <- resid_all %>%
  group_by(type, model) %>%
  summarise(
    bp_p = ifelse(n() > 3,
                  bptest(lm(residual ~ Yr, data = cur_data()))$p.value, NA),
    .groups = "drop")
```

```{r residualsummary, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
resid_stats %>%
  left_join(shapiro_p, by = c("type", "model")) %>%
  left_join(ljung_p,   by = c("type", "model")) %>%
  left_join(bp_p,      by = c("type", "model")) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 5))) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Summary statistics and residual diagnostic tests by type and model",
      digits = 5, align = "c", booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE, position = "center",
                latex_options = "HOLD_position") %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
```

### Spawning Biomass Comparison: s1.1 vs s1.4

```{r bio_stats}
spawn_bio_1 <- base.model1.1$timeseries$SpawnBio
spawn_bio_4 <- base.model1.4$timeseries$SpawnBio

shapiro.test(spawn_bio_1)
shapiro.test(spawn_bio_4)

leveneTest(c(spawn_bio_1, spawn_bio_4),
           group = rep(c("Model 1.1", "Model 1.4"),
                       each = length(spawn_bio_1)))

t.test(spawn_bio_1, spawn_bio_4, var.equal = TRUE)
wilcox.test(spawn_bio_1, spawn_bio_4)
```

Both tests strongly indicate that Model s1.1 and Model s1.4 produce
significantly different estimates of spawning biomass. Model s1.1
predicts higher values, suggesting that the ecosystem components in
s1.4 result in more conservative stock status estimates.

# REFERENCES
